a1.s1: Focus on Tier 1 ARIA vendors (320) for immediate case referrals — these have been independently flagged by the risk model, anomaly detection, and external registries.
a1.s2: Cross-reference with SAT EFOS ghost company registry (13,960 entities). Any active procurement relationship with a definitive-status EFOS vendor warrants immediate review.
a1.s3: Price volatility is the strongest signal — 99.9% detection rate on the IMSS ghost network. Prioritize vendors whose contract amounts swing wildly across institutions.

a2.s1: The AMLO administration shows 2.4× the high-risk rate of the Fox era — a structural shift worth quantifying by sector and institution in your coverage.
a2.s2: 48.6% of contracts show co-bidding patterns consistent with bid rings. Map the most frequent co-bidding clusters to surface potential cartels in your state or sector.
a2.s3: COVID-19 emergency procurement: 5,371 contracts, avg risk score 0.821. The pandemic-era case files offer a ready-made investigation track with public records.

a3.s1: Direct award rate peaked at 79.4% under AMLO — OECD limit is 25%. Bringing Mexico within the international benchmark would require structural legislative change.
a3.s2: Single-bid competitive procedures affect 16.5% of contracts (505,219) — a red flag masquerading as a competitive process. Demand minimum-bidder thresholds enforced in law.
a3.s3: Price outliers exceed sector median by more than 3× in 17,373 contracts. A mandatory price-reasonableness review above that threshold would recover billions in over-payments.
